) hat im Juli 2014 das Qualitätsstandard: ) launched the quality standard program IS-BAH in July 2014. IS-BAH stands for International Standard for Business Aircraft Handling and describes an internationally valid industry standard for handling processes in business aviation. The program is demanding and participants must undergo regular audits to maintain their IS-BAH certification.

Montreal, Kanada – Der „International St: Montreal, Canada – The “International Standard for Business Aircraft Operations (IS-BAO)” was launched in 2002. The IS-BAO, created specifically for the Business Aviation, is based on the standards and recommended processes of the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). The focus is on safety management in the areas of flight operations, administration, training and education of pilots as well as maintenance and technology. The standard is recognized by several national regulatory authorities.

Rheinmünster, Deutschland – ACM AIR CHAR: Rheinmünster, Germany – ACM AIR CHARTER signed two aircraft management contracts for two new Boeing BBJ2 in Europe in the first half of 2014. With two aircraft in the fleet, ACM AIR CHARTER is the largest operator of the BBJ2 type.
Rheinmünster, Deutschland – Aufgrund des: Rheinmünster, Germany - Due to the Part-NCC of Regulation (EU) No. 965/2012 (Air Ops) issued by the European Commission, which has been in force since August 25, 2016, operators of non-commercial aircraft are, to an adjusted extent, subject to the same requirements as commercial aviation companies. This presents many, especially private and smaller operators, with new, sometimes major, challenges. The law stipulates, among other things, that an operator of a non-commercial aircraft must create an operations manual (OM) and introduce a management system. Furthermore, the operator must confirm in a “declaration” to the Federal Aviation Office that he meets all the requirements of the NCC regulations and is aware of his responsibilities.
Rheinmünster, Deutschland – Das Luftfahr: Rheinmünster, Germany – The Federal Aviation Office has granted ACM AIR CHARTER approval to immediately monitor the continued airworthiness of the Cessna 510 (Citation Mustang) aircraft. The range of tasks includes, among other things, the creation and approval of maintenance programs, the management of Type Certificate (TC) Holder documents and the monitoring of airworthiness directives and operating times. This also includes planning upcoming maintenance measures to ensure continued airworthiness.
Rheinmünster, Deutschland – Der erste Bo: Rheinmünster, Germany – The first Bombardier Global 7500 has now been added to the managed aircraft fleet. The jet was delivered in January 2021 and was today approved for charter flights by the Federal Aviation Office (LBA) on the AOC (Aircraft Operating Certificate).
Rheinmünster, Deutschland – Mit dem vier: Rheinmünster, Germany - With the fourth aircraft in the Global Series from the Canadian aircraft manufacturer Bombardier, ACM AIR CHARTER now offers four ultra-long-haul jets on the charter market.
Rheinmünster, Deutschland – Mit der Über: Rheinmünster, Germany – By taking over ownership of a Dassault Falcon 2000LX, ACM AIR CHARTER now offers another aircraft in the medium-haul sector alongside the established Falcon 2000EX.
Rheinmünster, Deutschland – Vor sieben J: Rheinmünster, Germany – Seven years ago, ACM AIR CHARTER became the first commercial airline to receive IS-BAO certification in Germany. The certificate is based on an internationally recognized audit system for assessing safety-relevant operational processes and management systems of an airline. Back in 2014, ACM was finally awarded the highest possible “Level 3” certification. This award was again given to ACM as part of an independent safety and operational audit by the International Business Aviation Council in 2017.

“ACM AIR CHARTER ist ein Pionier und Bra: “ACM AIR CHARTER is a pioneer and industry leader in introducing the global IBAC security standards, and we are pleased to see how consistently and professionally these are implemented across the company,” said Kurt Edwards, General Manager of IBAC. „Exzellenz in der Luft, Vertrauen am Bod: “Excellence in the air, trust on the ground”
